metadata.description: A curated collection of AI agent skills from official development teams and the community.

skills.name: accessibility
skills.description: Audits and improves web accessibility against WCAG 2.
skills.whatItDoes: Audits and improves web accessibility against WCAG 2.1 guidelines. The skill analyzes web pages and code for accessibility issues, providing actionable recommendations and code fixes.
whenToUse: Auditing a web application for WCAG 2.1 compliance
Fixing accessibility issues in React components
Adding proper ARIA attributes to interactive elements
Improving keyboard navigation support
Testing screen reader compatibility
skills.skillMd:
---
name: accessibility
description: Audits and improves web accessibility against WCAG 2.
---

# Accessibility Skill

A skill for auditing and improving web accessibility against WCAG 2.1 guidelines.

## Usage

When asked to audit or fix accessibility issues, use this skill to:

1. Analyze the current state of accessibility
2. Identify WCAG violations
3. Provide specific code fixes
4. Verify improvements

## Guidelines

- Focus on WCAG 2.1 Level AA compliance
- Prioritize semantic HTML
- Ensure keyboard navigation
- Test with screen readers

---
name: agent-framework-azure-ai-py
description: Python SDK for building persistent agents on Azure AI Foundry.

# Azure AI Agent SDK

Python SDK for building persistent agents on Azure AI Foundry.

## Installation

```bash
pip install azure-ai-agents
```

## Usage

```python
from azure.ai.agents import Agent

agent = Agent()
await agent.run("Your task here")
```

skills.name: agents-md
skills.description: A skill for creating and maintaining AGENTS.md files — the documentation that agent tools like Claude read when working in a project.
skills.whatItDoes:
A skill for creating and maintaining AGENTS.md files — the documentation that agent tools like Claude read when working in a project. It enforces a minimal format: under 60 lines, headers and bullets only, file-scoped commands over full builds, and no duplication of what already lives in linter configs.

Writing AGENTS.md by hand tends to balloon into prose paragraphs and redundant linter rules — this skill enforces the research-backed constraint that instruction-following degrades past 100 lines.
whenToUse: Setting up AGENTS.md from scratch in a new project
Adding file-scoped typecheck and lint commands so agents don't run full builds
Writing commit attribution rules that agents follow when pushing code
Auditing an existing CLAUDE.md for bloat and trimming it to under 60 lines
Structuring a monorepo with subdirectory AGENTS.md overrides per package
skills.skillMd:
---
name: agents-md
description: A skill for creating and maintaining AGENTS.md files.
---

# Agents MD Skill

A skill for creating and maintaining AGENTS.md files.

## Purpose

AGENTS.md files document how AI agents should work in a project.

## Format

Keep AGENTS.md under 60 lines:

```markdown
# Project Name

## Commands
- `pnpm test` - Run tests
- `pnpm lint` - Lint code

## Guidelines
- Follow TypeScript conventions
- Write tests for new features
```

## Best Practices

1. Keep it concise
2. Use headers and bullets only
3. Focus on commands and conventions
4. Avoid duplicating linter configs

---
name: apollo-client
description: Build React applications with Apollo Client 4.
---

# Apollo Client Skill

Build React applications with Apollo Client 4.

## Setup

```javascript
import { ApolloClient, InMemoryCache } from '@apollo/client';

const client = new ApolloClient({
  cache: new InMemoryCache(),
  uri: 'https://api.example.com/graphql'
});
```

skills.name: apollo-federation
skills.description: Helps you write Apollo Federation 2.x subgraph schemas for composing multiple GraphQL APIs into a single supergraph. Covers entity definitions with @key, field sharing with @shareable, computed fields via @requires, and other federation directives.
skills.whatItDoes:
Helps you write Apollo Federation 2.x subgraph schemas for composing multiple GraphQL APIs into a single supergraph. Covers entity definitions with @key, field sharing with @shareable, computed fields via @requires, and other federation directives.

Federation directive rules are easy to get wrong, and composition errors are cryptic. Having the correct syntax, constraints, and patterns inline while editing schemas saves repeated trips to the docs.
whenToUse: Defining entity types with @key when splitting a monolith GraphQL API into subgraphs
Adding fields to an existing entity from a new subgraph without breaking composition
Setting up computed fields that depend on data owned by another subgraph
Debugging composition errors after changing federation directives
Migrating a field from one subgraph to another using @override
skills.skillMd:
---
name: apollo-federation
description: Helps you write Apollo Federation 2.
---

# Apollo Federation Skill

Helps you write Apollo Federation 2.x subgraph schemas.

## Key Directives

- `@key` - Define entity primary keys
- `@shareable` - Allow field to be shared across subgraphs
- `@requires` - Mark fields as required from another subgraph
- `@override` - Migrate fields between subgraphs

## Example

```graphql
type User @key(fields: "id") {
  id: ID!
  name: String!
  email: String @external
}
```

skills.name: minimax-pdf
skills.description: Generates print-ready PDFs through three routes: creating documents from scratch with a token-based design system, filling form fields in existing PDFs, and reformatting existing documents into styled output. Supports 15 cover styles with per-type typography and color tokens. Works with reports, resumes, proposals, portfolios, and more.
skills.whatItDoes:
Generates print-ready PDFs through three routes: creating documents from scratch with a token-based design system, filling form fields in existing PDFs, and reformatting existing documents into styled output. Supports 15 cover styles with per-type typography and color tokens. Works with reports, resumes, proposals, portfolios, and more.

Instead of manually wiring together PDF libraries, font loading, cover design, and form handling, this skill provides a single pipeline that handles design tokens, cover rendering, body layout, and merging in one command.
whenToUse: Generating a client proposal PDF with a split-panel cover and custom accent color
Filling out a government or legal form PDF without touching the layout
Converting a Markdown draft into a designed, print-ready report
Building a resume with an oversized typographic cover from structured content
Reformatting a plain text document into a magazine or editorial PDF layout
skills.skillMd:
---
name: minimax-pdf
description: Generates print-ready PDFs through three routes.
---

# MiniMax PDF Skill

Generates print-ready PDFs through three routes.

## Routes

1. **Create from scratch** - Use token-based design system
2. **Fill forms** - Fill existing PDF form fields
3. **Reformat** - Style existing documents

## Supported Types

- Reports
- Resumes
- Proposals
- Portfolios

## 15 Cover Styles

Each type has unique typography and color tokens.

Builds complete frontend pages by combining UI design, scroll animations, AI-generated media assets, and copywriting into a single workflow. Covers landing pages, marketing sites, dashboards, and product pages. Uses MiniMax APIs to generate images, video, audio, and music locally rather than relying on external stock assets.

Instead of coordinating separate tools for design, animation, asset creation, and copywriting, this skill runs all five phases in sequence with enforced quality gates, so you get a shippable page without manually stitching workflows together.

Generating background music and voiceover audio for a product demo page
Writing AIDA-structured conversion copy for a SaaS signup page

skills.name: doc-coauthoring
skills.description: A structured workflow for co-authoring documents with Claude. It guides users through three stages: context gathering, section-by-section drafting with iterative refinement, and reader testing using a fresh Claude instance to catch blind spots before sharing.
skills.whatItDoes:
A structured workflow for co-authoring documents with Claude. It guides users through three stages: context gathering, section-by-section drafting with iterative refinement, and reader testing using a fresh Claude instance to catch blind spots before sharing.

Rather than writing freeform and hoping the doc lands, this workflow catches gaps and ambiguities by testing the finished doc with a context-free Claude instance before anyone else reads it.
whenToUse: Drafting a technical spec by dumping meeting notes and letting Claude ask clarifying questions
Writing a decision doc with structured brainstorming for each section before committing to content
Testing a proposal against reader questions before sending it to stakeholders
Co-authoring an RFC where the author has context but needs help organizing it into a readable structure
Generating alt-text for images in a shared doc so Claude-assisted readers can understand the visuals
skills.skillMd:
---
name: doc-coauthoring
description: Structured workflow for co-authoring documents with Claude.
---

# Doc Co-authoring Skill

Structured workflow for co-authoring documents.

## Stages

1. **Context gathering** - Collect all relevant information
2. **Drafting** - Section-by-section with refinement
3. **Testing** - Use fresh Claude to catch blind spots

## Benefits

- Catches gaps before sharing
- Ensures clarity
- Tests with context-free reader

skills.name: design-md
skills.description: Analyzes screens in a Stitch project and writes a DESIGN.md file that captures the visual design system in natural language. It extracts color palettes, typography, component styles, and layout principles from screen HTML and metadata.
skills.whatItDoes:
Analyzes screens in a Stitch project and writes a DESIGN.md file that captures the visual design system in natural language. It extracts color palettes, typography, component styles, and layout principles from screen HTML and metadata, translating technical Tailwind values into descriptive terms Stitch can use when generating new screens.

Reading through HTML and Tailwind classes to reconstruct a coherent design language is slow and error-prone — this skill does it in one pass and produces a structured file Stitch accepts directly as prompt context.
whenToUse: Capturing a Stitch project's color roles and hex codes in a single reference file
Translating Tailwind border-radius and spacing classes into design-prompt-ready language
Documenting typography hierarchy from existing Stitch screen HTML before adding new screens
Building a shared DESIGN.md so multiple Stitch-generated screens stay visually consistent
Extracting shadow and elevation patterns from a designed screen for use in future prompts
skills.skillMd:
---
name: design-md
description: Analyzes Stitch screens and writes DESIGN.md files.
---

# Design MD Skill

Analyzes screens and writes DESIGN.md files.

## Output

- Color palettes
- Typography hierarchy
- Component styles
- Layout principles

## Format

Natural language design documentation.
